paper_title: Cellulose Extraction and Climate Data Analysis

methods.method_name: Cellulose Extraction and Oxygen Isotope Measurements
methods.description: This method involves the extraction of α-cellulose from tree rings and the measurement of oxygen isotopes to analyze historical climate data.

steps.description: We selected eight cores from eight trees (5 cores from HP, 3 cores from WL) with no absent rings and homogeneous growth patterns (interseries correlations greater than 0.5) for stable isotopes analysis, covering a period of 1815-2014.
steps.searchable_summary: select tree cores for isotope analysis

steps.description: We 'pool' the whole wood from their annual rings prior to α-cellulose extraction following standard pooling methodology.
steps.searchable_summary: pool wood from annual rings for cellulose extraction

steps.description: The 'pooling' method has been proven to be reliable at our sites according to high coherency of tree-ring cellulose δ 18 O data of four individual cores from four different trees during the period of 1950-2014.
Then α-cellulose of the annual tree rings were extracted following a modified method of [43] and [44].
In order to homogenize the cellulose, an ultrasonic water bath (JY92-2D, Scientz Industry, Ningbo, China) was used to break the cellulose fibers.
steps.searchable_summary: use ultrasonic water bath to homogenize cellulose

steps.description: The α-cellulose was then freeze-dried for three days prior to isotope analysis.
The tree-ring δ 18 O values were measured using a High Temperature Conversion Elemental Analyzer (TC/EA) coupled to a Finnigan MAT-253 mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure δ 18 O values using mass spectrometer

steps.description: Each cellulose sample was repeatedly measured four times to improve precision.
We calculated the mean isotopic values of each sample without outliers (values more than three standard deviations from the mean).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean isotopic values excluding outliers

steps.description: We measured the ratio for a benzoic acid working standard (repeated four times) with a known δ 18 O value every seven measurements to monitor the analytical precision and to calibrate the samples for analytical accuracy.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate samples using benzoic acid standard

methods.method_name: Climate and Tree-Ring Width Data Collection
methods.description: This method describes the collection and preparation of climate and tree-ring width data for analysis.

steps.description: The sampling sites (HP, 110.55˚E, 30˚N, 860m a.s.l.; WL, 110.67˚E, 29.83˚N, 893m a.s.l.), located in the middle Yangtze River basin, have a humid and warm subtropical monsoon climate.
steps.searchable_summary: identify sampling sites in Yangtze River basin

steps.description: The instrumental records from the nearest Shimen meteorological station, obtained from China Meteorological Administration, reveal climate data.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch climate data from Shimen meteorological station

steps.description: The EASM index used as a calibration data for reconstruction is defined as an area-averaged seasonally dynamical normalized seasonality of wind fields at 850 hPa within the East Asian monsoon domain.
In the fieldwork, two cores per tree were extracted using increment borers.
steps.searchable_summary: extract tree cores using increment borers

steps.description: The samples were mounted, air dried, polished and cross-dated based on the standard dendrochronology procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare tree cores using dendrochronology procedures

steps.description: Ring widths were measured to 0.01 mm accuracy and quality checked with moving correlations using the COFECHA program.
steps.searchable_summary: measure and quality check ring widths
